evidence: Round 1: BLOCKED because the D1 decision ended with two LF bytes and git show --check failed.
Round 2: BLOCKED because the still-allowlisted export template retained v1.5 Formal G1 and final-bundle paths while the launcher enforced v1.6 paths globally.
Round 3: NO_BLOCKER on commit 1d9b5dba after the export template paths were rebound while both SHA fields remained zero placeholders; git show --check passed.
findings.summary: The byte-level decision defect and export-template path incompatibility are resolved across the three-round ledger.

evidence: The round-3 delta is limited to the export template, its single candidate-manifest row and the workflow log.
The 70-row manifest is sorted, unique and valid at SHA-256 c2d1338c719966700c0002050cecfa5cf586f6c3cc64866000dc38d733ac2e75.

Launcher 494e483c44642717bb7a843d0e84d4d8b96f9dca0271299d1e60fc2224c6c994 at 117383 bytes and wrapper 6058f216c6d0d91245661b2278dd6f764f149a3c6a8ae54e646c1b6cfb5a44f7 at 8108 bytes remain coherently pinned.
findings.summary: Round-3 byte scope, manifest integrity and managed launcher pins are coherent.

evidence: The persisted round-3 execution report records 124 focused tests passed in a detached clone and no launcher or HPC operation.
No rating value was accessed and no HPC job was submitted during execution/governance review.
findings.summary: Execution/governance verification passed without crossing the submit or rating boundary.
